channel.title: kubeadm on Jung Yeon Park
channel.description: Recent content in kubeadm on Jung Yeon Park

item.title: 3. Kubeadm Init
item.description: This is the last of three posts will focus on how to bring up a kubeadm cluster in an air-gapped environment. Prepare for kubernetes Download required images for kubeadm Initialize Kubeadm [*] This post focuses on initializing kubeadm, install overlay networking, and join nodes to the initialized cluster. You should have: Five VMs (1 for master, 4 for workers) and prepared them for a kubeadm install Downloaded the required docker images Uploaded the images to a private repository Please refer to the first for information on how to set up k8s nodes and the second to download required images from the internet.

item.title: 2. Required images for kubeadm
item.description: This is the second of three posts will focus on how to bring up a kubeadm cluster in an offline environment. Refer to the first post for information on how to set up k8s nodes and to install kubeadm. Prepare for kubernetes Download required images for kubeadm [*] Initialize Kubeadm This post focuses on the required Docker images for kubeadm and downloading/uploading them to our private registry.

item.title: 1. Prepare offline kubeadm install
item.description: This is the first of three posts will focus on how to bring up a kubeadm cluster in an offline environment. I completed this a couple of months ago, when the latest k8s version was 1.9, but the concepts should translate over to newer versions. Prepare for kubernetes [*] Download required images for kubeadm Initialize Kubeadm Kubeadm is one of the tools used to create a Kubernetes cluster.
